title: [Backend/Search] Implement semantic search with embedding vectors
description:
## Context
AI-powered semantic search as an enhancement layer on top of keyword search (FTS). Allows searching by meaning rather than exact keywords.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Embedding model: configurable — local (Transformers.js in Node worker) or OpenAI text-embedding-3-small API
- [ ] Embedding generated on note save (BullMQ background job, debounced)
- [ ] Embeddings stored in pgvector extension (vector column on Note model) or separate vector table
- [ ] Semantic search endpoint: POST /search/semantic with { query, limit, threshold }
- [ ] Hybrid search: combine FTS score + cosine similarity score with configurable weight (default: 0.6 FTS, 0.4 semantic)
- [ ] Similar notes suggestions: GET /notes/:id/similar — returns top 5 semantically similar notes
- [ ] Semantic search toggle in search UI (switch between keyword and semantic)
- [ ] Embedding regeneration: admin endpoint POST /admin/reindex-embeddings to rebuild all
- [ ] Graceful degradation: if embedding service unavailable, fall back to FTS only
- [ ] Embedding dimensions: 384 (MiniLM) or 1536 (OpenAI) — configurable
- [ ] Chunk large notes into paragraphs for more precise matching

## Technical Notes
- pgvector extension: CREATE EXTENSION vector; column: embedding vector(384)
- Prisma: raw SQL for vector operations (cosine distance: <=> operator)
- BullMQ job: EmbeddingWorker processes note content on save/update
- Local model: @xenova/transformers with all-MiniLM-L6-v2
- Feature flag: SEMANTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED=true env var
close_reason: Implemented semantic search with embedding vectors. Added EmbeddingService (OpenAI, configurable provider, batch processing, ValKey cache), SemanticSearchService (cosine similarity via pgvector, FTS fallback), Prisma migration for note_embeddings table with pgvector IVFFlat index, GET /workspaces/:wid/search/semantic endpoint, updated SearchModule registration. 31 new tests (16 embedding + 15 semantic), all passing.

title: [Backend] Implement rate limiting middleware
description:
## Context
Rate limiting to protect all API endpoints from abuse.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] @nestjs/throttler configured globally
- [ ] Auth endpoints: 10 requests/minute per IP
- [ ] API endpoints: 100 requests/minute per user
- [ ] File upload: 20 requests/minute per user
- [ ] Custom rate limits injectable per route with @Throttle()
- [ ] ValKey as rate limit store
- [ ] 429 response with Retry-After header
- [ ] IP + user ID combined rate limiting
close_reason: Implemented ValKey-backed rate limiting with combined IP+user key. Created ValkeyThrottlerStorage (custom ThrottlerStorage using ioredis sorted sets for per-window counting). Created AppThrottlerGuard extending ThrottlerGuard: getTracker() combines IP+userId, throwThrottlingException() sets Retry-After header. Converted ThrottlerModule.forRoot to forRootAsync injecting VALKEY_CLIENT. Added 3 named throttlers: default (100/min), auth (10/min), upload (20/min). Applied @Throttle({ auth: {} }) to /auth/login, /auth/register, /auth/refresh. Applied @Throttle({ default: { limit: 20 } }) to POST /users/me/avatar.

title: [Backend/Auth] Implement JWT access and refresh token flow
description:
## Context
JWT-based session management with short-lived access tokens and long-lived refresh tokens.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JWT strategy configured with RS256 signing
- [ ] Access token: 15-minute TTL, stored in memory on client
- [ ] Refresh token: 7-day TTL, stored in httpOnly cookie
- [ ] POST /auth/refresh endpoint validates refresh token, issues new access token
- [ ] POST /auth/logout endpoint invalidates refresh token in DB
- [ ] JwtAuthGuard for protected routes
- [ ] CurrentUser decorator extracts user from JWT payload
- [ ] Token rotation on refresh
close_reason: Implemented RS256 asymmetric JWT signing alongside HS256 fallback. Updated auth.module.ts to configure JwtModule with RS256 when JWT_PRIVATE_KEY + JWT_PUBLIC_KEY env vars are present. Updated jwt.strategy.ts to use public key for verification. Updated token.service.ts to sign with RS256 algorithm when keys are configured. Fixed refresh token TTL default from 30 days to 7 days (604800s) matching spec. Fixed cookie max-age to 7 days. Added JWT_PRIVATE_KEY and JWT_PUBLIC_KEY config env vars.

title: [Backend/Notes] Implement file watcher for external changes
description:
## Context
Watch the filesystem for changes made outside the application (e.g., direct git push, SSH edits).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] chokidar file watcher configured for workspace storage paths
- [ ] Detects: file add, file change, file delete, directory changes
- [ ] Debounce rapid changes (500ms) before processing
- [ ] On change: re-read file, update DB metadata, re-index for search
- [ ] On delete: soft-delete note in DB
- [ ] Ignores: hidden files, node_modules, temp files
- [ ] Per-workspace watcher instances
- [ ] Handles watcher cleanup on workspace deletion
close_reason: Wired file watcher into the notes pipeline. Created FileWatcherService that subscribes to FilesService 'fileChange' events. Handles: add/change (read content from disk, upsert Note DB record with title/contentHash/wordCount/frontmatter, re-index via SearchService.indexNote); unlink (soft-delete note isTrashed=true, remove from search index via SearchService.removeFromIndex). Content hashes are compared before writing to avoid redundant DB updates. Events are debounced per (workspaceId, relativePath) with 500ms window using @notesaner/utils debounce. Non-markdown files and directory events are ignored. External adds where workspace has no OWNER are skipped with a warning. FileWatcherService added to NotesModule with SearchModule imported.

If different: external change detected, re-index and notify via WebSocket. File watcher also updates hash on change. Atomic hash update: update hash and content together. API response includes contentHash for client-side change detection.
close_reason: 61 tests, SHA-256 hashing, constant-time comparison, batch validation, external change detection

title: [Refactor/FSD] Restructure Next.js app/ and src/ for proper FSD layers

Inside src/ create FSD layers: app/ (providers, global setup), pages/ (page compositions), widgets/ (Sidebar, Header, EditorPanel), features/ (user interactions), entities/ (Note, Workspace, User, Tag), shared/ (api, lib, ui, config, hooks). Route files in root app/ import from src/pages/. This is the core structural change.
close_reason: FSD restructuring complete. app/ moved to project root (routing only). src/ now has proper FSD layers: app (providers/styles), pages (14 page compositions), widgets (3 shells), features (unchanged), entities (placeholder), shared (unchanged). Zero new TS errors.

title: [Backend/Search] Implement full-text search with PostgreSQL tsvector
description:
## Context
Full-text search engine using PostgreSQL native FTS capabilities.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GIN index on notes.search_vector tsvector column
- [ ] Trigger or application-level update of search_vector on note save
- [ ] GET /workspaces/:wId/search?q= endpoint
- [ ] Ranked results using ts_rank
- [ ] Highlighted context snippets using ts_headline
- [ ] Searches: title, content, tags, frontmatter properties
- [ ] Minimum query length: 2 characters
- [ ] Unit tests for search ranking
close_reason: Implemented full-text search with PostgreSQL tsvector. All acceptance criteria met: GIN index + trigger in migration SQL, FTS ranked results via ts_rank_cd, ts_headline snippets, 4-weight indexNote (title/content/tags/frontmatter), 2-char minimum enforcement at both DTO and service layers, class-validator DTOs, controller endpoints for search/suggest/index/rebuild, 20 unit tests all passing.

Features: strikethrough via ns-task-item--done CSS class on checked items, data-checked attribute for CSS targeting, nested tasks (nested:true), getTaskListStats() for completion percentage, formatTaskListStats() for status bar display. title: [Backend/DevOps] Implement automated backup and disaster recovery
description:
## Context
Automated backup strategy for PostgreSQL database and filesystem note storage to prevent data loss.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Automated PostgreSQL backup via pg_dump on configurable schedule (default: daily at 02:00 UTC)
- [ ] Filesystem backup: tar archive of workspace note directories
- [ ] Backup retention policy: keep last 7 daily, 4 weekly, 3 monthly
- [ ] Backup stored to configurable destination: local filesystem, S3-compatible storage
- [ ] Backup verification: restore test to temporary DB on weekly schedule
- [ ] Manual backup trigger via admin API: POST /admin/backups
- [ ] Backup status endpoint: GET /admin/backups (list, size, date)
- [ ] Restore procedure documented and scripted
- [ ] Backup encryption with configurable key (AES-256)
- [ ] Alert on backup failure (via SMTP email to admin)

## Technical Notes
- BullMQ scheduled job for automated backups
- pg_dump for PostgreSQL, tar for filesystem
- S3 upload via @aws-sdk/client-s3 (MinIO compatible)
- Store backup metadata in PostgreSQL (backup_logs table)
- Docker volume considerations for backup storage
close_reason: Closed

title: [Docs] Analyze documentation structure from analogues (Obsidian, Notion, Logseq docs)
description: Research documentation sites of analogues: Obsidian (help.obsidian.md), Notion (notion.so/help), Logseq (docs.logseq.com), Docusaurus itself. Extract common structure patterns: getting started, guides, API reference, plugin development, self-hosting. Produce a documentation sitemap/outline for Notesaner. Output: docs/DOCS_STRUCTURE.md with proposed sections, page hierarchy, and rationale.

title: [Backend] Configure CORS and security headers
description: HTTP security configuration for all API responses. CORS: allow list based on ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var. Helmet.js: sets all recommended security headers. CSP headers for HTML responses (strict for API, allow iframes for plugins). HSTS: Strict-Transport-Security header. X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Permissions-Policy for sensitive browser APIs.
close_reason: Configured CORS and security headers. Enhanced Helmet with explicit CSP (strict default-src: none, allow frameSrc/connectSrc for self/plugins), HSTS production-only (max-age=1y, includeSubDomains, preload), X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, COEP/COOP/CORP cross-origin isolation. Added Permissions-Policy middleware for camera, microphone, geolocation, payment, usb etc. Added ThrottlerModule to AppModule with two named throttlers: default (100 req/60s) and auth (10 req/60s), with ThrottlerGuard registered globally. CORS now trims whitespace from ALLOWED_ORIGINS entries.

title: [DevOps] Create production Docker images (multi-stage builds)

Tested with docker-compose production config.
close_reason: Created multi-stage production Dockerfiles: apps/server/Dockerfile (4-stage: base/deps/builder/runner, health check on /health, non-root nestjs user, notes volume, exposes 4000) and apps/web/Dockerfile (4-stage: base/deps/builder/runner, Next.js standalone output, non-root nextjs user, health check on /api/health, exposes 3000). title: [Backend/Sync] Implement Yjs document conflict resolution strategy
description: Handle conflicting edits when reconnecting after offline period. CRDT guarantees no data loss (Yjs). Server canonical state from DB applied as base. Client offline changes merged via Yjs CRDT algorithm. No manual conflict resolution required for text. For metadata (frontmatter): last-write-wins per field. Conflict log: record merge events for audit. Test: simulate two clients editing offline, reconnect, verify merge.
close_reason: Closed

title: [Graph] Implement WebGL graph rendering for large vaults (>1000 nodes)

WebGL canvas for hardware-accelerated rendering. Maintains 60fps with 10,000+ nodes. All interactivity preserved: click, hover, zoom, pan. Progressive loading: render nearest nodes first. Performance benchmark: 1000 nodes render in <1s.

title: [Frontend/Workspace] Implement floating/detachable windows
description:
## Context

- [ ] Minimize floating window to taskbar/ribbon area
- [ ] Multiple floating windows supported simultaneously
- [ ] Z-index management: clicking a floating window brings it to front
- [ ] ESC key or close button dismisses floating window (returns to grid)
- [ ] Floating windows are still part of the global tab bar (buffer tabs)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+Shift+F to toggle float on active pane

## Technical Notes

title: [Experimental] Custom component overrides for admin users (Monaco/VSCode integration)
description:
EXPERIMENTAL — Scheduled for post-MVP, after all core tasks are complete.

Allow admin-role users to override the implementation of individual UI components within their workspace. Users get the base component source as a starting point and can customize behavior, layout, and styling.

## Vision
Admin users should be able to:
1. View the source code of any UI component (read-only base)
2. Fork/override a component for their workspace
3. Edit the override using an embedded code editor (Monaco/VSCode Web)
4. See live preview of changes before applying
5. Revert to the base component at any time
6. Share component overrides with other workspaces

## Acceptance Criteria

### Component Override System
- [ ] Component registry: list all overridable components with their props/API
- [ ] Override storage: per-workspace, stored server-side
- [ ] Override format: JSX/TSX with limited imports (React, shadcn/ui, Notesaner SDK)
- [ ] Sandboxed execution: overrides run in iframe sandbox with message API
- [ ] Version pinning: overrides pin to a base component version
- [ ] Breaking change detection: flag when base component API changes
- [ ] Rollback: one-click revert to base implementation

### Code Editor Integration
- [ ] Embedded Monaco editor with TypeScript support
- [ ] IntelliSense for Notesaner component API, props, and SDK
- [ ] Type checking in real-time
- [ ] Live preview panel (split view: code + rendered output)
- [ ] Diff view: base vs override
- [ ] OR: Deep link to VSCode Web with workspace mounted
- [ ] OR: Local VSCode extension that syncs overrides

### Overridable Components (initial set)
- [ ] NoteCard: note preview card in lists
- [ ] FileTreeItem: file explorer row
- [ ] StatusBarItem: status bar segments
- [ ] SidebarPanel: sidebar panel containers
- [ ] ToolbarButton: toolbar button groups
- [ ] CalloutBlock: callout/admonition rendering
- [ ] CodeBlock: code block rendering
- [ ] SearchResultItem: search result row

### Security
- [ ] Overrides cannot access: localStorage, cookies, network, DOM outside sandbox
- [ ] CSP policy for override iframes
- [ ] Admin-only: workspace OWNER/ADMIN role required
- [ ] Audit log: all override changes tracked
- [ ] Rate limiting on override saves (prevent abuse)

### Architecture
- [ ] Component SDK: @notesaner/component-sdk with types, hooks, utilities
- [ ] Override compiler: server-side SWC/esbuild to compile TSX → JS
- [ ] Override CDN: compiled overrides served with cache headers
- [ ] Fallback: if override fails to render, auto-revert to base with notification

## Technical Notes
- This is a COMPLEX feature requiring careful sandboxing and security review
- Consider Web Components for isolation boundary
- Monaco editor: @monaco-editor/react for embedded editing
- VSCode Web: explore code-server or vscode.dev integration
- Override API: POST /workspaces/:id/component-overrides/:componentId
- Priority P4 (backlog) — implement only after all P0-P3 tasks are complete
- Requires dedicated security review before launch
- Consider feature flag: ENABLE_COMPONENT_OVERRIDES=false by default

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Plugin SDK being stable, theming system complete, security audit done

title: [Frontend/Workspace] Implement global tab bar for all open buffers
description:
## Context
CRITICAL UX REQUIREMENT: The tab bar must be GLOBAL — a single unified bar at the very top of the workspace area (below the app header, above all panes). It shows ALL open buffers/tabs regardless of which pane they are displayed in.

This CORRECTS the behavior in notesaner-ed8 (closed) and notesaner-vad (closed) which described per-pane tab bars. The new model is:
- ONE global tab bar at the top
- Clicking a tab opens that buffer in the CURRENTLY FOCUSED pane
- Tabs do NOT belong to individual panes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single unified tab bar rendered at the top of the workspace center area
- [ ] Tab bar shows ALL open buffers across the entire workspace
- [ ] Clicking a tab opens/focuses that buffer in the currently active (focused) pane
- [ ] Tab bar does NOT attach to individual panes — it is workspace-global
- [ ] Tabs show: note title, dirty indicator (dot), close button (X)
- [ ] Reorder tabs via drag-and-drop (dnd-kit)
- [ ] Right-click context menu: Close, Close Others, Close All, Close Saved, Copy Path, Reveal in File Explorer
- [ ] Ctrl/Cmd+W closes active tab
- [ ] Ctrl/Cmd+Tab cycles through tabs (forward)
- [ ] Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Tab cycles through tabs (reverse)
- [ ] Middle-click on tab closes it
- [ ] Pin tab: pinned tabs are smaller (icon-only) and stick to the left
- [ ] Tab overflow: horizontal scroll with left/right arrows when tabs exceed width
- [ ] New tab button (+) at the right end opens quick switcher
- [ ] Active tab visually highlighted (matches focused pane content)
- [ ] Double-click empty space in tab bar creates new untitled note
- [ ] Drag tab out of tab bar area to open in new pane (split)
- [ ] Tab count badge or indicator when many tabs open

## Technical Notes
- Refactor TabConfig in layout-store: remove panelId from tabs, tabs are global
- Add activePaneId to layout-store to track which pane is focused
- Tab click handler: set the focused pane's noteId to the clicked tab's noteId
- Sidebars do NOT have buffer/tab navigation — only the central area has tabs
- Tab state: { id, noteId, title, isPinned, isDirty }
- Pane state: { id, gridArea, type, focusedNoteId }
- The relationship is: pane.focusedNoteId points to a tab's noteId
close_reason: Closed

title: [Backend] Implement CDN and static asset caching strategy
description:
## Context
Production caching strategy for static assets, images, and API responses to improve performance and reduce server load.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Static assets (JS, CSS, fonts): Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable (content-hashed filenames)
- [ ] Note attachments/images: Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400 with ETag validation
- [ ] API responses: Cache-Control: private, no-cache with ETag for conditional requests
- [ ] ETag generation for note content (based on content hash or updatedAt)
- [ ] 304 Not Modified responses for unchanged resources
- [ ] CDN-ready: all static assets served from /_next/static with long cache
- [ ] Image assets served with Accept-based content negotiation (WebP when supported)
- [ ] ValKey page cache for SSR/SSG published note pages (5-minute TTL)
- [ ] Cache invalidation: bust ValKey cache on note update
- [ ] Nginx proxy_cache configuration for reverse proxy deployments
- [ ] Compression: gzip and brotli for text-based responses

## Technical Notes
- Next.js built-in static asset hashing handles JS/CSS caching
- NestJS: interceptor for ETag/Cache-Control headers on API responses
- ValKey: cache published page HTML with key page:{slug}:{path}
- Nginx config included in docker-compose setup
close_reason: Closed

title: [Backend/Security] Implement Content Security Policy and security hardening
description:
## Context
Production security hardening beyond basic CORS and headers (notesaner-ay8a, closed). CSP, subresource integrity, and security best practices.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Content Security Policy (CSP) header configured with strict rules
- [ ] CSP: script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: blob:; connect-src 'self' wss:
- [ ] Plugin iframes: sandbox attribute with allow-scripts, CSP restricts to plugin origin
- [ ] Subresource Integrity (SRI) for external scripts (if any)
- [ ] X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- [ ] X-Frame-Options: DENY (except plugin iframes)
- [ ] Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
- [ ] Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
- [ ] Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
- [ ] Cookie security: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict for auth cookies
- [ ] CSRF protection: SameSite cookies + CSRF token for state-changing POST/PUT/DELETE
- [ ] Security headers configurable via environment variables
- [ ] helmet middleware properly configured in NestJS

## Technical Notes
- Use @nestjs/common helmet integration
- CSP nonce generation for inline scripts if needed
- Plugin sandbox: iframe sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' with additional CSP
- Test headers with Mozilla Observatory
close_reason: Closed

title: [Advanced] Implement note export (Markdown, PDF, HTML, DOCX)
description:

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Export formats: Markdown (.md), HTML (.html), PDF (.pdf), DOCX (.docx)
- [ ] Markdown export: clean MD with all Notesaner-specific syntax resolved to standard MD
- [ ] HTML export: self-contained HTML with inline CSS, images embedded as base64
- [ ] PDF export: server-side generation via Puppeteer or client-side via print CSS + window.print()
- [ ] DOCX export: via docx library with heading styles, lists, tables, code blocks preserved
- [ ] Attachment bundling: option to include referenced images in a zip
- [ ] Batch export: select multiple notes in file explorer -> Export All -> zip containing all notes
- [ ] Vault export: export entire workspace as a zip (preserving folder structure)
- [ ] Custom filename template: configurable pattern e.g., {{title}}-{{date}}.{{ext}}
- [ ] Export preserves: frontmatter, wiki links converted to relative links, tags

## Technical Notes
- Backend: POST /notes/:id/export?format=md|html|pdf|docx returns file stream

title: [Backend] Implement rate limiting and abuse prevention
description:
## Context
Comprehensive rate limiting strategy beyond the basic middleware (notesaner-1ts, closed). This covers per-endpoint tuning, abuse detection, and IP-based throttling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global rate limit: 100 requests/minute per authenticated user
- [ ] Auth endpoints (login, register, password reset): 5 requests/minute per IP
- [ ] Search endpoint: 30 requests/minute per user
- [ ] File upload: 10 requests/minute per user, 50MB max per file
- [ ] WebSocket connections: max 5 per user
- [ ] Rate limit headers in responses: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
- [ ] 429 Too Many Requests response with Retry-After header
- [ ] IP-based throttling for unauthenticated endpoints
- [ ] Account lockout after 10 failed login attempts (30-minute cooldown)
- [ ] Rate limit configuration via environment variables (overridable per deployment)
- [ ] Rate limit state stored in ValKey (Redis) with TTL expiry
- [ ] Admin API to view rate limit status per user: GET /admin/rate-limits/:userId

## Technical Notes
- @nestjs/throttler with custom ValKey storage adapter
- Different ThrottlerGuard configurations per route group
- ValKey keys: ratelimit:{userId}:{endpoint}, ratelimit:ip:{ip}:{endpoint}
- Sliding window algorithm for smoother rate limiting

title: [Backend] Implement health check and readiness endpoints
description: Kubernetes-ready health and readiness probes. GET /health - liveness probe: returns 200 if process alive. GET /health/ready - readiness probe: checks DB connection, ValKey connection, filesystem writable. Response format: { status: ok, checks: { db: ok, cache: ok, fs: ok } }. Response time < 100ms. title: [Frontend/Workspace] Implement dual sidebars with drag-and-drop panel rearrangement
description:
## Context
CRITICAL UX REQUIREMENT: Both left and right sidebars must support panel rearrangement — users can drag panels between sidebars and reorder panels within a sidebar. Sidebars do NOT have buffer/tab navigation; that belongs exclusively to the central area's global tab bar.

This EXTENDS the behavior in notesaner-xrk, notesaner-0wx, and notesaner-qz2 (all closed). Those tasks established the basic sidebar structure. This task adds the drag-rearrange capability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Left sidebar: collapsible, default panels: File Explorer, Search, Bookmarks/Favorites, Tags
- [ ] Right sidebar: collapsible, default panels: Outline, Backlinks, Properties, Comments
- [ ] Both sidebars collapse/expand independently (Cmd+[ for left, Cmd+] for right)
- [ ] Panels within each sidebar can be reordered via drag-and-drop
- [ ] Panels can be dragged FROM one sidebar TO the other sidebar
- [ ] Drop zone indicators appear when dragging a panel over a sidebar
- [ ] Panel order persisted in user preferences (sidebar-store)
- [ ] Each panel has a collapse/expand toggle (accordion-style within sidebar)
- [ ] Multiple panels can be expanded simultaneously within a sidebar
- [ ] Sidebars do NOT contain buffer tabs — no tab bar in sidebars
- [ ] Panel headers show: icon, title, collapse chevron, optional action buttons
- [ ] Plugin-registered sidebar panels can be dragged like built-in panels
- [ ] Sidebar resize handles (drag left/right edge to resize sidebar width)
- [ ] Minimum sidebar width: 200px, maximum: 50% of viewport
- [ ] When sidebar has no panels (all dragged away), show empty state with hint
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut to cycle active panel within sidebar: Tab key when sidebar focused

## Technical Notes
- Extend sidebar-store.ts: replace fixed leftActiveTab/rightActiveTab with ordered panel arrays
- Panel config: { id: string, type: PanelType, sidebar: 'left' | 'right', order: number, collapsed: boolean }
- Use dnd-kit for cross-sidebar drag-and-drop
- Panel types registered via a registry pattern (allows plugin panels)
- Serialize panel arrangement in user preferences API
- Keep file explorer tree and search as separate panel components
close_reason: Closed

title: [Frontend/Workspace] Implement free-form window grid system with drag, resize, and custom layouts
description:
## Context
CRITICAL UX REQUIREMENT: Replace the current horizontal/vertical-only split pane system with a full free-form grid system. Users must be able to build arbitrary grid layouts, not just simple splits.

This task SUPERSEDES the behavior defined in notesaner-vad (split pane) and notesaner-92q (snap layouts). Those tasks were closed with a simpler split model. This task upgrades the system to a true grid.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can drag panes to any position in a CSS Grid-based layout
- [ ] Users can resize panes freely by dragging borders/dividers
- [ ] Users can rearrange panes by dragging pane headers to new grid positions
- [ ] Custom grid layouts supported: not limited to horizontal/vertical splits
- [ ] Grid supports arbitrary row/column arrangements (e.g., 2x2, 1 large + 2 small, L-shaped)
- [ ] Panes snap to grid positions when dragged near grid lines (snap threshold: 20px)
- [ ] Drop zone indicators appear when dragging a pane over valid positions
- [ ] Save and restore custom window grid configurations (named layouts)
- [ ] Saved layouts persist server-side (Layout model) and locally (localStorage fallback)
- [ ] Minimum pane size enforced (200px width, 150px height)
- [ ] Grid dividers are draggable to adjust column/row sizes
- [ ] Layout picker (Cmd+Shift+L) shows preset and saved layouts
- [ ] Preset layouts: single, 50/50, 70/30, 3-column, 2x2, 1+2 (main + two side)
- [ ] Each pane tracks its focused note via the layout-store
- [ ] Pane focus indicated visually (subtle border highlight on active pane)
- [ ] Keyboard navigation between panes: Ctrl+Arrow keys

## Technical Notes
- Extend existing layout-store and snap-layout-types.ts
- CSS Grid (grid-template-columns, grid-template-rows) for layout rendering
- Consider react-grid-layout or custom CSS Grid implementation
- Pane state: { id, gridArea, type, focusedNoteId }
- Grid config serialized as: { columns: string, rows: string, areas: PaneArea[] }
- IMPORTANT: Panes do NOT have their own tab bars — see global tab bar task
- Integration with layout-persistence.ts for server sync
close_reason: Closed

close_reason: Upgraded error handling and logging. AllExceptionsFilter now produces RFC 7807 Problem Details (application/problem+json) with type URI, title, status, detail, instance, code, requestId, timestamp fields. 400 responses include errors[] array listing failing fields from class-validator. 401 responses set WWW-Authenticate: Bearer header. 500 errors log full stack, never expose it. - [ ] Admin override: per-workspace quota override in admin panel
- [ ] Storage recalculation: BullMQ cron job (daily at 03:00 UTC) recalculates accurate totals
- [ ] Incremental tracking: update counters on note create/delete/update for real-time approximation

## Technical Notes
- WorkspaceStorageStats table: workspaceId, totalBytes, noteCount, attachmentCount, versionCount, calculatedAt
- Incremental counters in ValKey: storage:{workspaceId}:bytes (updated on each file operation)
- Daily cron reconciles ValKey counter with actual filesystem/DB calculation
- Filesystem calculation: du -sb on workspace directory
- DB calculation: SUM(length(content)) + SUM(file_size) from attachments

- [ ] Registers: "ay yank to register a, "ap paste from register
